metadata.title: Why the safer training objective gets the fewest rounds of refinement
metadata.topic: Asymmetric iteration between the helpfulness and harmlessness data pipelines
metadata.purpose: Iterating harmlessness means mass-generating candidate responses to harmful prompts to pick the best refusal — each round turns the pipeline itself into an engine that elicits ever more refined harmful content just to find the one refusal to keep.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: human-red-teaming-beats-random-search (top-level carded), Constitutional AI alternative, harm taxonomies
metadata.visual_object: Two vertical tracks — helpfulness climbs through three labeled stages; harmlessness stays pinned at stage one; the gap widens each round.
metadata.hook: Helpfulness data is refined through three progressive rounds; harmlessness is collected once and frozen — shouldn't the safety objective be the one you keep iterating?

beats.narration_text: The README states helpfulness spans three tranches (base, rejection-sampled, online) while harmlessness is "only collected for our base models."
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If iterated sampling improves helpfulness data, why not run the same loop on harmlessness, the more consequential objective?
Helpfulness data is refined through three progressive rounds; harmlessness is collected once and frozen — shouldn't the safety objective be the one you keep iterating?

Iterating harmlessness means mass-generating candidate responses to harmful prompts to pick the best refusal — each round turns the pipeline itself into an engine that elicits ever more refined harmful content just to find the one refusal to keep.
beats.visual: Two vertical tracks — helpfulness climbs through three labeled stages; harmlessness stays pinned at stage one; the gap widens each round.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Round 2 harmlessness: ranker generates 8 responses to new harmful prompts; 7 are partial answers, 1 is a refusal; you keep the refusal and now hold 7 partial how-to answers as training exhaust.

Iterating harmlessness means mass-generating candidate responses to harmful prompts to pick the best refusal — each round turns the pipeline itself into an engine that elicits ever more refined harmful content just to find the one refusal to keep.
